entry-1.section: Florida diminished value recovery distinction by claim type
entry-1.content: Florida common law permits recovery of diminution in value as tort damages for harm to vehicles, measured as the difference between pre-harm and post-harm fair market value or reasonable repair costs plus any residual loss. First-party insurance policies, however, do not require payment for inherent diminished value when the insurer elects repair or replacement under standard collision coverage language.
entry-1.source_problem: Florida automobile collision claims seeking diminished value recovery, either in third-party tort actions against an at-fault driver or insurer, or under first-party own-policy collision coverage
